name: ElmSyntaxTypeInfer

unions.name: Base10Or16
unions.comment:
Either decimal or hexadecimal.
Used by [`PatternInt`](#Pattern) and [`ExpressionInteger`](#Expression)

unions.name: Expression
unions.comment:
Like [`Elm.Syntax.Expression.Expression`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Expression#Expression)
but its sub-nodes are [`TypedNode`](#TypedNode)s.

Be aware when trying to match references that they are split into multiple variants:
  - `ExpressionReference` (for let/module declared value/function or port or pattern variable use)
  - `ExpressionReferenceVariant`
  - `ExpressionReferenceRecordTypeAliasConstructorFunction`
cases: { moduleOrigin : String.String, qualification : String.String, name : String.String }
{ moduleOrigin : String.String, choiceTypeName : String.String, qualification : String.String, name : String.String }
{ moduleOrigin : String.String, qualification : String.String, name : String.String }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }
{ record : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, fieldNameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, fieldName : String.String }

{ operator : { moduleOrigin : String.String, symbol : String.String, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type }, left : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, right : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ condition : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, onTrue : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, onFalse : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ called : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, argument0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, argument1Up : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression) }
List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ recordVariable : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode { moduleOrigin : String.String, name : String.String }, field0 : { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }, field1Up : List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression } }

{ parameter0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, parameter1Up : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ matched : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, case0 : { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }, case1Up : List.List { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression } }

{ declaration0 : { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, declaration : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.LetDeclaration }, declaration1Up : List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, declaration : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.LetDeclaration }, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

unions.name: LetDeclaration
cases: { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, expression : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression }

{ name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, signature : Maybe.Maybe { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationType : Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation, annotationTypeRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range }, parameters : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type }

unions.name: Pattern
unions.comment:
Like [`Elm.Syntax.Pattern.Pattern`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Pattern#Pattern)
but all its sub-nodes are [`TypedNode`](#TypedNode)s
cases: { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, variable : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode String.String }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern }
{ head : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern, tail : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern }
{ moduleOrigin : String.String, choiceTypeName : String.String, qualification : String.String, name : String.String, values : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern) }

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
Type information attached to expressions and patterns,
see [`TypedNode`](#TypedNode).

This is different from [`Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-TypeAnnotation#TypeAnnotation)
in that it doesn't contain
information unrelated to type inference like ranges, qualification levels or parens.

Importantly, it also contains the overarching range
of all places where each specific type variable is used.
This is useful to for example find out whether a variable in a let declaration type
is "forall" or inherited from the surrounding context
(if the use range is fully included in the let declaration range it's forall, otherwise inherited).

unions.name: TypeNotVariable
cases: { moduleOrigin : String.String, name : String.String, arguments : List.List ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type }
{ recordVariable : { useRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String }, fields : FastDict.Dict String.String ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type }

aliases.name: ModuleOriginLookup
aliases.comment:
How to map names and symbols used in a module to their origin module.
Create with [`importsToModuleOriginLookup`](#importsToModuleOriginLookup).

Contains variants, type alias names, choice type names, port names, value/function declaration names
and whether `(|.)` and or `(|=)` are imported from `Parser.Advanced`.

Also contains locally declared names when available.

Type- and expression/pattern-level members
are separated since you can for example have a local `List` variant
while also using an (implicitly) imported `List` type.
aliases.type: { references : FastDict.Dict String.String (FastDict.Dict String.String String.String), typeConstructs : FastDict.Dict String.String (FastDict.Dict String.String String.String), keepOperatorIsExposedFromParserAdvanced : Basics.Bool, ignoreOperatorIsExposedFromParserAdvanced : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: ModuleTypes
aliases.comment:
Known types of members in a module.
Create with [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
and [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)
aliases.type: { signatures : FastDict.Dict String.String ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, typeAliases : FastDict.Dict String.String { parameters : List.List String.String, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, recordFieldOrder : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String) }, choiceTypes : FastDict.Dict String.String { parameters : List.List String.String, variants : FastDict.Dict String.String (List.List ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type) } }

aliases.name: TypedNode
aliases.comment:
A part in the syntax tree with an attached

  - [range](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Range#Range) in the source
  - global [`Type`](#Type): concretely inferred,
    influenced by other branches and value/function (let) declaration type annotations
aliases.type: { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, value : value }

values.name: elmCoreTypes
values.comment:
[`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes) exposed in `elm/core`.

Please _always_ start with [`elmCoreTypes`](#elmCoreTypes)
and add further module info with [`FastDict.union`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/miniBill/elm-fast-dict/latest/FastDict#union)
using [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
and [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)

And if for some reason you already know used dependencies at compile time,
you can re-use the [code generator used for these elm/core types](https://github.com/lue-bird/elm-syntax-type-infer/tree/main/codegen)

values.name: importsToModuleOriginLookup
values.comment:
Calculate valid mappings of qualifications + name
to origin module based on a module's imports, see [`ModuleOriginLookup`](#ModuleOriginLookup).

Requires all exposed [`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes)
so we can resolve `exposing (..)` and `ChoiceType(..)`.
values.type: FastDict.Dict String.String ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes -> List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Import.Import) -> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup

values.name: moduleDeclarationsToTypes
values.comment:
Extract all known types
from declarations within a module.
Un-annotated value/function declarations are ignored.

For dependency modules, use [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)
values.type: { moduleOriginLookup : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup, moduleName : String.String } -> List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration) -> { types : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, errors : List.List String.String }

values.name: moduleInterfaceToTypes
values.comment:
Extract all known types
from module [metadata](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/project-metadata-utils/latest/),
usually from parsing the `docs.json` of a dependency.

For elm-syntax modules, use [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
values.type: Elm.Docs.Module -> { types : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, errors : List.List String.String }

values.name: valueAndFunctionDeclarations
values.comment:
Infer types of
value/[`Elm.Syntax.Expression.Function`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Expression#Function) declarations
in a module.

    import Elm.Syntax.Node
    import Elm.Syntax.Expression
    import ElmSyntaxTypeInfer


    [ { declaration =
            Elm.Syntax.Node.empty
                { name = Elm.Syntax.Node.empty "hello"
                , expression =
                    Elm.Syntax.Node.empty
                        (Elm.Syntax.Expression.Literal "world")
                , arguments = []
                }
      , signature = Nothing
      , documentation = Nothing
      }
    ]
        |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.valueAndFunctionDeclarations
            { importedTypes = ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.elmCoreTypes
            , moduleOriginLookup = exampleModuleOriginLookup
            , otherModuleDeclaredTypes =
                []
                    |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.moduleDeclarationsToTypes
                        exampleModuleOriginLookup
                    |> .types
            }
    -->
    Ok
        [ { name = "hello"
          , type_ =
                ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypeNotVariable
                    (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypeConstruct
                        { moduleOrigin = [ "String" ], name = "String", arguments = [] }
                    )
          ...
          }
        ]


    exampleModuleOriginLookup : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup
    exampleModuleOriginLookup =
        []
            |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.importsToModuleOriginLookup
                ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.elmCoreTypes

See [`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes) and [`ModuleOriginLookup`](#ModuleOriginLookup)

Warning! Always first verify that provided type alias declarations aren't (mutually) recursive.
Otherwise this function might never terminate.
values.type: { moduleName : String.String, importedTypes : FastDict.Dict String.String ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, moduleOriginLookup : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup, otherModuleDeclaredTypes : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes } -> List.List Elm.Syntax.Expression.Function -> Result.Result String.String (List.List { parameters : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, documentation : Maybe.Maybe { content : String.String, range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range }, signature : Maybe.Maybe { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationTypeRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationType : Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation } })
